80 years. Every top mathematician alive tried this problem. Nobody solved it.

An internal OpenAI reasoning model did it in a single attempt.

9 of the world's top mathematicians verified the proof. A Fields Medalist said he'd recommend it for publication "without any https://t.co/biXMkGpNMe

The problem, posed by Paul Erdős in 1946, sounds like a children's puzzle.

Put dots on a flat surface. How many pairs of dots can you place exactly one unit apart?

For 80 years, mathematicians believed a square grid was basically the best answer. Thousands tried to prove it.

Here's the part that should surprise you:

The model wasn't built for math. It's a general-purpose reasoning system. No special training for this problem. No custom tools. No human steering it toward the answer.
